March 15, 2024

[Project Owner or Owner's Representative Name]
[Company Name]

[City, State ZIP Code]

RE: Formal Notice of Claim under Contract [Contract Number] – [Project Name]

Dear [Owner's Representative Name],

We represent [Contractor Name], the general contractor for the [Project Name], pursuant to Contract No. [Contract Number] between [Contractor Name] and [Project Owner], dated [Contract Date]. In accordance with the notice provisions of Section [Specific Contract Clause], we hereby provide formal notice of claim.

The triggering event for this claim is the [description of event, e.g., "unforeseen differing site conditions"], which was first identified on [Exact Date of Event] as per our communications. The occurrence of this event has resulted in a significant impact on the project's critical path, necessitating both an extension of time and additional monetary compensation.

Preliminary analysis indicates an estimated time extension requirement of [X days/weeks/months] and additional costs totaling [Preliminary Cost Figure] to account for labor, materials, and associated delay impacts. These figures are based on current evaluations and are subject to further details being confirmed and finalized.

In submitting this notice, [Contractor Name] expressly reserves all rights and remedies under the contract and applicable law. We anticipate discussing this matter further and will provide any necessary documentation to substantiate this claim as the evaluation process continues.

Please regard this notice as our intent to resolve the matter amicably and expediently, aligned with the contractual obligations of both parties.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this notice. We look forward to your acknowledgement of receipt and a timely response as stipulated in our contract provisions.

Sincerely,

David R. Castillo, Esq.
Castillo & Associates, P.A.

title: Deposition Summary Memo
content:
MEMORANDUM

TO: [Attorney Name]

FROM: [Your Name]

DATE: [Current Date]

RE: Deposition Summary of Raymond Castillo - Bayshore Tower Project

Executive Summary

This memorandum provides a summary of the deposition of Raymond Castillo, Senior Superintendent at Meridian Construction Group, concerning issues on the Bayshore Tower project. Key topics include the improper timing of waterproofing membrane application, the necessity for physical remediation, and details surrounding Change Order No. 11.

Witness Background

Raymond Castillo serves as a Senior Superintendent at Meridian Construction Group, with oversight responsibilities on the Bayshore Tower project. His duties involved ensuring construction standards and verifying the correction of identified defects.

Key Admissions

  • Castillo admitted the application of the waterproofing membrane occurred prior to the full curing of the concrete. (Tr. 5)
  • He acknowledged that the condition of water infiltration was not consistent with a properly installed waterproofing system, necessitating physical remediation. (Tr. 9)
  • Change Order No. 11 pertained to additional waterproofing work on the balconies. (Tr. 11)

Key Denials

Castillo denied characterizing Meridian's charges as efforts for remediation of defective installations and relied on assurances from another supervisor for defect corrections without personal documentation.

Exhibits Discussed

  • Meridian Daily Log dated August 12, 2022
  • RFI No. 47 and Response
  • Photographs of balcony slab, Unit 1204
  • Change Order No. 11
  • Castillo's resume

Inconsistencies Noted

  • There were inconsistencies in Castillo's timeline awareness regarding the waterproofing issue by June 3, 2022.
  • Despite having verification duties, Castillo largely depended on another supervisor's assurances for defect correction.

Recommendations

Conduct further inquiries into the verification processes utilized by Mr. Castillo and Meridian Construction Group regarding defect corrections. Additionally, a deeper review of Change Order No. 11’s scope should be pursued to fully understand its implications and completion.


Prepared by: [Your Name]
disclaimer: This output is not legal advice and requires attorney review before any action is taken.

title: Draft Claim Letter
content:
ABC Law Firm
123 Legal Lane
Suite 456
Miami, FL 33101
Phone: (305) 555-0123
Email: info@abclawfirm.com

October 5, 2023

[Project Owner Name]
[Company Name]
[Street Address]
[City, State, ZIP]

RE: Bayshore Tower Condominium, Contract No. [Contract Number] – Notice of Claim for Change Order 11 Costs

Dear [Project Owner],

Pursuant to Section [Contract Notice Clause] of the contract for the Bayshore Tower Condominium, Meridian Construction Group, LLC hereby provides formal notice of a claim arising from Change Order Number 11, issued on October 4, 2022. The triggering event for this claim is the non-compliant installation of balcony waterproofing membranes identified on September 28, 2022, necessitating additional work for correction as reflected in the Change Order.

The impact of this non-conformance has resulted in additional costs amounting to $86,930. This cost stems from the necessary removal and reinstallation of the waterproofing membrane to achieve compliance with the manufacturer's requirements, as outlined in Change Order No. 11. Notably, while the Change Order confirms there is no impact on the project’s substantial completion date, the financial implications are substantial and require compensation.

This letter serves as preliminary quantification of the cost impact and formal invocation of the right to additional compensation as prescribed under the contract. Meridian Construction Group, LLC expressly reserves all rights for time extensions and additional compensation under the contract and under applicable law.

If there are any questions regarding this notice, please contact me directly at the details provided above.

Sincerely,

[Attorney Name]
ABC Law Firm
123 Legal Lane
Suite 456
Miami, FL 33101
Phone: (305) 555-0123
Email: info@abclawfirm.com

If so, what can you tell us about them? - Did you have any involvement in responding to RFIs or claim notices? - Have you had any interactions regarding payment disputes or defect discoveries on the project?

title: Completed Draft Summary
content:
MEMORANDUM

TO: [Attorney Name]

FROM: [Your Name]

DATE: [Today's Date]

RE: Internal Case Summary for Keystone Building Group, Inc. - Palmetto Ridge Medical Office Building Project

Project Overview

Project Name: Palmetto Ridge Medical Office Building

Location: 4850 Military Trail, Palm Beach Gardens, FL 33418

Owner: Palmetto Ridge Development, LLC

General Contractor: Keystone Building Group, Inc.

Key Subcontractors: Information not explicitly available in provided documents

Contract Amount: $24,750,000.00

Contract Type: Standard Form of Agreement (specific form type not provided)

Project Scope: The undertaking involves the construction of a medical office building,including every aspect of structural, mechanical, and electrical components outlined in the project's specifications.

Dispute Overview

The dispute centers on delay claims related to the Palmetto Ridge Medical Office Building project. Keystone Building Group, Inc., claims delays due to late responses to RFIs, owner-directed design changes, and delayed delivery of owner-furnished equipment. Keystone seeks a contract extension of 94 days and additional compensation totaling $1,847,000.00. The Owner, Palmetto Ridge Development, LLC, has denied the claim, citing untimeliness and contractual defenses, including no damages for delay except in specific circumstances.

Parties

Owner: Palmetto Ridge Development, LLC

General Contractor: Keystone Building Group, Inc.

Counsel for Owner: Not identified in provided documents

Counsel for Contractor: Not identified in provided documents

Relevant Contract Terms

Required Notice: The Contractor must inform the Owner and Architect in writing of any claims for extended time or additional costs within a 14-day timeframe from the occurrence of the event (01_Prime_Contract_Extract.txt). Procedures for Extending Time: Details regarding the process for submitting and handling these are not specified, except for the initial notice requirement (01_Prime_Contract_Extract.txt).

Dispute Resolution Clauses: Covered in the contract but specifics are not quoted or detailed (01_Prime_Contract_Extract.txt).

Liquidated Damages: $4,500 per calendar day for delays beyond substantial completion, capped at $600,000 (01_Prime_Contract_Extract.txt).

Retainage Provisions: Not explicitly detailed in the provided documents.

Chronology

April 12, 2023: Contract executed (01_Prime_Contract_Extract.txt).

May 1, 2023: Notice to proceed issued (01_Prime_Contract_Extract.txt).

October 17, 2023: RFI-089 issued regarding a structural conflict (02_RFI_089_and_Log.txt).

November 14, 2023: Delayed response to RFI-089 received (02_RFI_089_and_Log.txt).

February 29, 2024: Schedule Update No. 11 indicates project is 94 days behind (03_Schedule_Update_11_Narrative.txt).

March 22, 2024: Notice of Claim letter by Keystone dated (04_Notice_of_Claim_Letter.txt).

April 15, 2024: Owner's Denial Letter issued (05_Owner_Denial_Letter.txt).

Document Gaps

While the core allegations and defenses are detailed in the available documents, there are significant gaps including:

- Specific contractual references that might support Keystone's claims for a contract extension and additional compensation (03_Schedule_Update_11_Narrative.txt).

- Delivery dates for owner-furnished equipment are not confirmed (03_Schedule_Update_11_Narrative.txt).

- Detailed impact analysis for cost and time related to project delays (02_RFI_089_and_Log.txt).

- Justifications for the $1,847,000 claim amount (04_Notice_of_Claim_Letter.txt).

title: Draft Claim Letter

Bayshore Tower Condominium Association

c/o [Attorney's Firm Name]
[Attorney's Address]
[Attorney's Phone Number]
[Attorney's Email]

[Date]

[Owner's Name]
[Owner's Company]
[Owner's Address]

RE: Bayshore Tower Condominium Project - Contract No. [Contract Number] - Formal Notice of Claim

Dear [Owner's Name],

Pursuant to the contract provisions set forth in section [Relevant Contract Clause], Bayshore Tower Condominium Association hereby provides formal notice of a claim arising from defective waterproofing membrane installation by Meridian Construction Group, LLC, under Change Order Number 11, dated October 4, 2022. The triggering event for this claim was the identification of installation issues by Meridian's superintendent on September 28, 2022, as reflected in the project's documentation.

This notice addresses the need for additional work following the improper application of the waterproofing membrane on balcony slabs, which failed to comply with the manufacturer's installation requirements. As a result, Bayshore Tower Condominium Association incurs additional costs in the amount of $86,930, as specified in Change Order No. 11. While there is no noted schedule extension affecting the project's critical path, the financial impact on the project requires compensation and attention.

Preliminary quantification of the claim is as follows: Additional compensation in the amount of $86,930 due to the requirement for removal and reinstallation of the membrane that did not conform to standards initially.

Bayshore Tower Condominium Association expressly reserves all rights under the contract and applicable law to recover all costs, expenses, and damages incurred as a result of this matter. This claim notice is issued without prejudice to any additional or further claims that may arise from related or subsequent issues under the construction contract.

We await your prompt acknowledgment of this claim and look forward to a resolution consistent with the contractual obligations and industry standards.

Sincerely,

[Attorney's Name]
[Attorney's Firm Name]
[Attorney's Firm Address]
[Attorney's Phone Number]
[Attorney's Email]

title: Draft Client Update
content:
```html

To: [Client Email]

From: [Attorney Name]

Subject: Update on Bayshore Tower Construction Dispute

Dear [Client Name],

I am writing to provide you with an update on the ongoing construction dispute involving Bayshore Tower Condominium and Meridian Construction Group, LLC. Significant developments have occurred recently, and I want to ensure you have the latest information.

The key issue remains the non-compliance in the initial installation of the waterproofing membrane on the balconies. Change Order Number 11, issued on October 4, 2022, detailed necessary additional work to correct these installation issues, resulting in an additional cost of $86,930, but no change to the project's substantial completion date. The issue was initially identified on September 28, 2022.

We have gathered testimony from Raymond Castillo, a Senior Superintendent with Meridian, who admitted awareness of the installation defects as early as August 2022 but failed to document the defects as required, which has been a point of contention. His deposition further highlighted discrepancies in documenting and verifying corrective actions, with conflicting statements regarding the timeline of his awareness of the issue.

We are currently not aware of any immediate deadlines or hearings, but it is crucial to continue monitoring communications from Meridian and address any additional requests or correspondence that may arise.

There are a few action items we need you to complete:

  • Review the details of Change Order No. 11 to understand the impact on project costs.
  • Prepare any documents or records you might have regarding the project timeline and communications with Meridian that could support your position.
  • Let me know if there are any particular aspects or documents you would like further information on, so I can ensure those are prioritized in our review process.

Please let me know if you have any questions or need further clarification on any of these points. We will continue to monitor the situation closely and keep you updated on any further developments or required actions.

Best regards,

[Attorney Name]
[Law Firm Name]
[Law Firm Address]
[Law Firm Phone Number]

```

title: Draft Claim Letter
content:
Bayshore Legal Group, LLP
123 Coastal Avenue
Miami, FL 33132
(305) 555-0101
contact@bayshorelegalgroup.com

October 5, 2023

Meridian Construction Group, LLC
234 S. Construction Blvd.
Miami, FL 33130

RE: Bayshore Tower Condominium Project — Contract No. [Contract Number] — Formal Notice of Claim for Additional Costs due to Non-compliant Waterproofing Installation

Dear [Owner's Representative Name],

We represent the Bayshore Tower Condominium Association concerning the ongoing construction project at 1800 North Bayshore Drive, Miami, Florida. In accordance with the contract's notice provisions, specifically Section [Contract Clause Number related to change orders and claims], we hereby provide formal notice of a claim due to the non-compliant installation of balcony waterproofing membranes as issued in Change Order No. 11 dated October 4, 2022.

The triggering event for this notice was identified in the deposition of Raymond Castillo, a Senior Superintendent with Meridian Construction Group, where it was confirmed that awareness of the waterproofing installation problems occurred around August 2022, primarily due to premature application contrary to manufacturer requirements as outlined in RFI No. 047. The deficiency necessitated additional waterproofing work on the balconies to bring the installation up to the required standards.

This non-compliance resulted in additional costs amounting to $86,930, as detailed in Change Order No. 11. While there has been no extension to the project's substantial completion date as per your admission, this additional expenditure directly affects the project's financial framework.

In light of these circumstances, we are hereby preliminarily quantifying the impact as follows:

  • Cost Impact: $86,930
  • Schedule Impact: Zero days

Please consider this letter as full reservation of all rights of the Bayshore Tower Condominium Association under the contract, as well as applicable laws, to seek all remedies available for the recovery of expenses incurred and any other resulting damages due to the non-conformance with the installation specifications.

We await your acknowledgment of this claim notice within the requisite period as stipulated in our contract.

Sincerely,

[Attorney Name]
Bayshore Legal Group, LLP

Mr. Tom Brennan
Meridian Construction Group, LLC
456 Industry Drive

RE: Bayshore Tower Condominium Project — Contract No. [Contract Number] — Notice of Claim Related to Change Order No. 11

Dear Mr. Brennan,

We represent the Bayshore Tower Condominium Association in relation to the Bayshore Tower project. Please consider this letter as formal notice of a claim pursuant to [Specific Contract Clause for Notice], with respect to the issues surrounding the balcony slab waterproofing at Bayshore Tower.

The triggering event for this claim is the issuance of Change Order No. 11 dated October 4, 2022. The change order addresses deficiencies related to the initial installation of the waterproofing membrane, which did not comply with manufacturer requirements. According to the records, Meridian Construction Group's failure to ensure the compliance of the initial application necessitated removal and reinstallation work, as acknowledged by Raymond Castillo, Senior Superintendent, during his deposition on March 14, 2025.

The impact of this non-compliance is two-fold: it has resulted in added costs amounting to $86,930 as stipulated in Change Order No. 11. While the change order states no effect on the project's substantial completion date, this notice reserves the right to claim any time extension should future analysis determine an effect on the project schedule beyond current assessments.

The Bayshore Tower Condominium Association hereby reserves all rights to seek further compensation or remediate delays attributable to the issues outlined herein, under the applicable provisions of our contract and the governing law. We kindly request that you provide formal acknowledgment of this claim and any related documentation in due course.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. We look forward to resolving this issue amicably and in a timely manner.

Sincerely,
[Attorney Name]
Law Offices of [Attorney Name]

title: Cross Examination Outline
content:
Waterproofing Installation Non-compliance

Goal: Establish the timeline and awareness of the non-compliant waterproofing installation.

  1. Mr. Castillo, on what date did you become aware of problems with the waterproofing installation? [August 2022]
  2. Is it accurate to state that the issue regarding the membrane's premature application was first recorded in your daily log? [Yes]
  3. Did you observe these defects before the completion of any corrective action? [Yes]

Documentation and Reporting of Defects

Goal: Highlight failures in documenting and reporting the known defects in the waterproofing process.

  1. Is it part of your responsibilities as a superintendent to document non-conforming work with a report? [Yes]
  2. Despite being aware of the defects, did you write any non-conformance reports at the time the issues were discovered? [No]
  3. Did anyone else on the team document these defects formally before the change order was issued? [No]

Schedule Pressures and Early Application

Goal: Illustrate the effect of schedule pressures on the early application of the waterproofing membrane.

  1. Was the project tracking behind schedule as of August 12, 2022? [Yes]
  2. Did the schedule delay influence the decision to apply the waterproofing membrane before the concrete cured? [Yes]
  3. Was premature application instructed or approved as a corrective measure to align with project timeframes? [Yes]

Role and Verification of Remediation Work

Goal: Confirm the lack of personal verification performed for remediation work and challenge the reliance on secondary sources for confirmation.

  1. Mr. Castillo, did you personally verify that the waterproofing defects were corrected? [No]
  2. You mentioned relying on payment records instead of personal verification; is that correct? [Yes]
  3. Were these records from another department, such as accounting, rather than a construction inspection report? [Yes]

RFI No. 47 and Manufacturer's Requirements

Goal: Confirm awareness and adherence to the manufacturer's installation requirements as addressed in RFI No. 47.

  1. Were you involved in the issuance of RFI No. 47 regarding the waterproofing detail conflict? [Yes]
  2. Did the architect's response to RFI No. 47 confirm following the manufacturer's requirements? [Yes]
  3. Despite this guidance, was the installation carried out in accordance with these manufacturer-prescribed standards? [No]

title: Draft Defect Summary
content:
Bayshore Tower Condominium Association

On August 12, 2022, according to the Daily Log, a premature application of the waterproofing membrane occurred on floors 12 and 13. This application violated the RFI No. 47 requirements, which specified that concrete substrates must be allowed to cure for a minimum of 28 days or achieve 75% design strength before treatment. This non-compliance possibly compromised the structural integrity of the waterproofing, necessitating further repairs and costs.

Defect Summary Memorandum

Category: Waterproofing Defects

Project Location: Bayshore Tower Condominium, Balconies and Balcony-to-Wall Transitions

Condition Description: The waterproofing membrane installed on the balconies did not comply with the manufacturer's instructions, requiring removal and reinstallation due to inadequate application procedures. The conflict exists between the architectural detail and manufacturer's requirements for the balcony-to-wall transition, specifically regarding the upturn height and termination exposure.

Date of Discovery/Documentation: Initial installation issue identified by the superintendent on September 28, 2022. RFI regarding detail conflict issued on June 3, 2022, with an architect's response on June 13, 2022.

Responsible Party: Meridian Construction Group, LLC, with specific responsibility noted to the Senior Superintendent, Raymond Castillo, who recognized the issue in August 2022.

Documents Evidencing the Defect:

  • Change Order 011 - Additional waterproofing work details

  • Deposition of Raymond Castillo - Acknowledgment of premature application and defect documentation issues

  • Daily Log dated August 12, 2022 - Application of membrane before concrete curing

  • RFI 047 Waterproofing Detail - Conflict in installation requirements

Testing Findings, Expert Conclusions, or Repair Estimates: Change Order details the additional costs of $86,930 for the compliance modification work. No specific testing results or expert conclusions provided in the documents.

Code Compliance Issues: The need to follow the manufacturer's requirement for at least 28-day curing or 75% design strength of the concrete substrate prior to membrane application reflects compliance concerns.

Document Support: Change Order 011, Deposition of Raymond Castillo, Daily Log 2022-08-12, RFI 047 Waterproofing Detail

Additional Notes: Discrepancies were noted in the witness deposition regarding awareness and documentation timelines, reflecting inconsistencies in defect reporting procedures.

```
disclaimer: This output is not legal advice and requires attorney review before any action is taken.
